title: TenirWsMessages
description: Single source of truth for the tenir WebSocket message contract between the clients (TS) and the FastAPI api (Python). TS types and Pydantic models are generated from this file; do not hand-edit the generated outputs. Audio is sent as raw BINARY WebSocket frames (16kHz, signed 16-bit little-endian, mono PCM); every JSON frame below is a control or result message.

MicSource.title: MicSource
MicSource.description: Which microphone the client is currently capturing from.

Lang.title: Lang
Lang.description: Languages the STT engine may detect/report on captions.

CueLevel.title: CueLevel
CueLevel.description: How eagerly the api surfaces private context cues, chosen by the user in the client UI: 'conservative' (only unambiguous factual references), 'balanced' (clear questions/references), 'aggressive' (anything lookup-worthy). Sent on session.start; governs cue generation for that session.

SessionStart.title: SessionStart
SessionStart.description: Client -> server. Opens a live session before any audio frames are sent.

sessionId.description: Optional client-provided id to resume a dropped session; the server echoes the authoritative id in session.ready.

MicSwitch.title: MicSwitch
MicSwitch.description: Client -> server. Runtime microphone source change.

SessionEnd.title: SessionEnd
SessionEnd.description: Client -> server. Finalizes the session; the transcript and retained audio are persisted.

Ping.title: Ping
Ping.description: Client -> server. Liveness probe; server replies with Pong.

t.description: Client send timestamp (ms epoch), echoed back.

SessionReady.title: SessionReady
SessionReady.description: Server -> client. Session accepted; client may begin streaming audio.

CaptionPartial.title: CaptionPartial
CaptionPartial.description: Server -> client. Fast, unstable hypothesis for the live caption band.

CaptionFinal.title: CaptionFinal
CaptionFinal.description: Server -> client. Stable finalized segment with timing.

Cue.title: Cue
Cue.description: Server -> client. A private contextual info card the api derived from the live conversation (e.g. someone asks how far the sun is; the answer appears). Cues are private to the listener, not part of the conversation. Clients show it in a bordered box above the live transcript for ~10s, and persist it inline in the transcript history at atMs.

cueId.description: Stable id for this cue (dedupe / popup keying).

title.description: 1-3 word label for the cue box.

body.description: The contextual detail shown in the box / history popup.

atMs.description: Position on the conversation timeline (ms) the cue relates to, so history can render it inline where it appeared.

ErrorMessage.title: ErrorMessage
ErrorMessage.description: Server -> client. Recoverable or fatal error for the current session.
